title: Fantastic Beasts: The Crimes of Grindelwald | Exit Survey (Ep. 99)
format: Sean Fennessey drags the two Binge Mode hosts in to litigate the second Fantastic Beasts, fandom versus canon, and whether the movie works for anyone who hasn't read the books.

segments.heading: Exit Survey: 'The Crimes of Grindelwald'
segments.note: with Jason Concepcion and Mallory Rubin

films.title: Fantastic Beasts: The Crimes of Grindelwald
films.note: The movie on trial. Fennessey found it a confusing, un-special fantasy; Concepcion and Rubin argue it splits the difference between newcomers and fans and satisfies neither, with the Elder Wand reduced to fan service and the Credence-is-a-Dumbledore reveal threatening the canon. Praise reserved almost entirely for Jude Law's Dumbledore.

films.title: Fantastic Beasts and Where to Find Them
films.note: The first film, which Rubin loved and Fennessey found well-made but unnecessary. Its Obscurus reveal gets re-read as the key to Ariana Dumbledore's backstory.

films.title: The Mummy
films.note: Fennessey's opening parable: Alex Kurtzman calling the Tom Cruise reboot 'not for critics, for the fans' of a movie that had no fans, and what fan service really means.

films.title: The Golden Compass
films.note: Fennessey's reference point for a fantasy that's fine but doesn't pierce the broader audience.

films.title: Harry Potter and the Sorcerer's Stone
films.note: Invoked for Nicolas Flamel and the philosopher's/sorcerer's stone, dropped into 'Grindelwald' with no explanation.

films.title: Harry Potter and the Goblet of Fire
films.note: Where Nifflers first turn up, in Hagrid's care-of-magical-creatures lesson.

films.title: Harry Potter and the Half-Blood Prince
films.note: The cave scene Rubin reads as Dumbledore reliving his family's destruction.

films.title: Harry Potter and the Deathly Hallows: Part 1
films.note: Fennessey's favorite of the series for its low-stakes hangout stretch in the tent.

films.title: Harry Potter and the Deathly Hallows: Part 2
films.note: Named alongside Part 1; the source of the Ariana and King's Cross material the hosts keep returning to.

films.title: Rogue One: A Star Wars Story
films.note: Fennessey's closest comparison: a well-made prequel that satisfies the appetite for content without being special.

films.title: Star Wars: The Last Jedi
films.note: Cited for the 'not my Skywalker' backlash as a warning about franchise ownership and fan revolt.

Ads: Villains (Ringer podcast promo, opening ad read that references Silence of the Lambs)
Books & plays: Harry Potter and the Cursed Child (play)
Fantastic Beasts and Where to Find Them (Hogwarts textbook)
